title: Carduus obvallatus
intro: Carduus obvallatus, also known as the spotted thistle, is a biennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Carduus obvallatus include cardenolides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Carduus obvallatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Carduus obvallatus is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family.
